rationale: ADR-fitness and gate-safety are clean. Verified on disk: ADR-0037/0018/0023/0025 are ALL Accepted, and their declared Domain(s) jointly cover every impacted domain (workflow←0037/0023/0025; core←0018; execution←0037/0025; context-system←0018), so checkADRCoverage yields coveredAccepted for all four → adr-coverage passes at every bead-complete. Each cite intersects an impacted domain → no adr-cite-irrelevant. ADR-0022 (Proposed) is correctly NOT cited; no domain depends on it. The plan's ADR Fitness section maps coverage correctly and the 'extend ADR-0022, no supersede' reasoning is defensible (a prepended flat tier refines, not reverses, a still-Proposed resolution order). Gate-safety on the moves: Bead 5's flat-tree ownership read is protected because Bead 5 depends_on Bead 2, which lands the ref-anchored LoadOwnershipAtRef/domainManifestRelPath multi-prefix matchers + project-docs/** classification + self-glob update (verified workflow+core self-claim manifests) in Phase 1 before any move; Bead 3 claims internal/layout/**. Critically, ADR-0039 is created Proposed (create.go) but UNCITED, so coverage never becomes coveredProposedOnly — no adr-divergence-proposed hard-block at impl-approve.

findings.issue: Verified: divergence.go reads plan-frontmatter adr_citations (the four Accepted ADRs), NOT the spec's ADR Touchpoints, so ADR-0022's Proposed status in spec.md touchpoints is harmless. coverageOf returns coveredAccepted for all four impacted domains; adr-divergence-proposed/uncovered/unowned cannot fire on the cited set. ADR-0039 created via mindspec adr create lands Status: Proposed but is uncited, so it neither covers nor blocks — the plan correctly does not require flipping it to Accepted.

Bead 5 creates ADR-0039 to 'formalize' the flat layout-v2, but mindspec adr create defaults Status: Proposed and the plan is silent on ADR-0039's terminal status. Gate-safe (uncited → no proposed-block), but a Proposed ADR-0039 describing an already-shipped live layout is a governance inconsistency that could mislead a future reader / `mindspec adr list --status proposed`. Recommend the plan state ADR-0039 lands Accepted (or explicitly record it stays Proposed until final-review flip, mirroring spec-105). Not a blocker.

context-system's complete-gate coverage rests solely on ADR-0018 (Lean Bootstrap — glossary/policies removal). Mechanically valid: ADR-0018 declares context-system and budgeter.go is context-system-owned → coveredAccepted. But the Bead-2 budgeter layout change is only loosely related to ADR-0018's subject; ADR-0018's real fit is the Bead-5 vestigial drops. This is intrinsic to the domain-level (not change-level) coverage gate and passes cleanly — informational only, since ADR-0018 is Accepted and declares the domain.

The ADR-0037 amendment note lands in Bead 5, while the panel.json relocation behavior it documents lands in Bead 4. doc-sync (ADR-0031) gates code↔domain-overview/interfaces sync, not ADR amendments, so Bead 4 landing the spec-scoped panel scan ahead of the ADR-0037 amendment does not trip a gate. Noted as a sequencing observation; confirm Bead 4's panel/complete code change does not require a workflow domain-doc update in the same bead.
